{"step_by_step_thinking": "Swyer syndrome is a condition where individuals are XY karyotype but develop female external genitalia due to a mutation in the SRY gene. They typically have underdeveloped or absent ovaries. Given this, they would likely present with primary infertility as their ovaries are not functioning properly. Surrogacy could be an option, but it is not a direct treatment for the condition. Ova donation might be an option for those who want to have biological children, but it is not a definitive statement about the syndrome. Gonadectomy is not universally indicated for all patients with Swyer syndrome.", "answer_choice": "C"}
